message.content:
Explore the project structure at C:\Users\User to understand:
1. The full directory tree (especially src/, pages/, services/, types/, components/)
2. The App.tsx file - especially the union type for views, routing, sidebar, command palette, breadcrumb
3. The existing patterns in pages/Home.tsx, pages/Account.tsx, pages/EeatBuilder.tsx
4. The services/eeat/eeat-auditor.ts file for service patterns
5. The components/ScoreRing.tsx component
6. The types/ directory
7. The services/ directory structure

Give me the FULL content of these critical files:
- App.tsx
- pages/Home.tsx (first 100 lines and last 50 lines to understand structure)
- pages/Account.tsx (first 100 lines)
- pages/EeatBuilder.tsx (first 150 lines)
- services/eeat/eeat-auditor.ts (first 100 lines)
- components/ScoreRing.tsx
- Any existing types files

Focus on identifying:
- The exact ViewType union type definition
- How sidebar items are structured
- How lazy loading is done
- How the command palette entries work
- How breadcrumbs are configured
- The credit/gas deduction pattern
- The tab pattern used in EeatBuilder and Account

content.text: I'll systematically explore the project structure to understand the codebase patterns and architecture.
I'll explore the project structure and provide you with the critical file contents. Let me start by examining the directory tree and then reading the specific files you mentioned.
